Ramses claims that the beacon near our camp is part of a sort of security system that was used at some point in the past, before Heitor arrived and completely barricaded access to the fortress. Apparently the Kryptis had little fear of attack, as I hear it is not a particularly powerful weapon and has remained unused for some time—ignored by Heitor, and possibly scorned by Ignaxious. I suppose when one is able to create seemingly impregnable magical barrier defenses on a grand scale, such defenses become obsolete.    —Researcher Selly

Rata Novus golems have joined the Rata Novus lane to help collect stray ley energy.

Rata Novus has made substantial advancements since our last observation. There has been a significant leap forward in the mass automation of their society, and all but the most sophisticated work is now handled by golems. Additionally, they have not experienced a chak attack in decades, as their domestication and control of the creatures have been further cemented.Conclusion: The fractal has proven worthwhile already, and there are a number of new technologies they've developed that may be useful for our purposes. Our estimates indicate the city is likely to continue prospering and developing into the future. Recommend continuing the experiment indefinitely.

Rather than allow myself to sit by a stream and mourn the lost... Push to my feet, I must. The most earnest homage I can pay to my fallen comrades is to move forward in our pursuit against the strangers. "Eyes to the horizon," she always urged. Windstruck would hate to see me on my rump with teary eyes.    The bearkin have long developed a salve to combat various illnesses; honey, dandelions, spruce resin, celery, and crushed cabbage will eliminate a number of ailments to the body and earth. This concoction has served as a good foundation in our efforts here, given some of the components' soothing of anxiety. A few more herbs and a bit of salt from the bay and we've been able to make it properly ingestible—though, savory it is not. The riled journeykin gag at the taste, so I advise priming the medicine with some sort of meat. For smaller or more wild creatures that are not used to interacting with the bearkin or outsiders, we should subdue them before administering the remedy to prevent undue stress. I'll see if anyone can help me with this exploration.    Progress has been a steady wade through springtime currents, but each step brings us closer to an antidote. My soul may heal after.
